items.title: Internal test patterns moved onto `b.testing`
items.body: Existing `auditCaptured` and `fakeNow` ad-hoc test patterns across the framework are migrated to the new `b.testing` primitive so every test file uses the same shared helpers instead of rolling its own clock/audit fakes.

items.title: `b.testing` primitive
items.body: A shared testing primitive landed at `b.testing` that bundles the framework's recurring test helpers (deterministic clocks, audit capture, fake request/response shapes) so tests across the codebase can share them instead of each file recreating its own mocks.

items.title: `b.notify` primitive
items.body: Outbound notification dispatch (email, SMS gateway, webhook, push) is consolidated behind one primitive with per-channel adapters, throttling, and audit emission â€” applications no longer wire each transport directly.

items.title: `b.i18n` primitive
items.body: Internationalization (message catalogs, locale resolution from request, pluralization, formatting) is a first-class primitive â€” applications stop wiring their own message-bundle loader and locale negotiator.

items.title: `b.seeders` primitive
items.body: Database/storage seeders are now a first-class primitive â€” operators declare seed data alongside the application and the framework runs idempotent boot-time provisioning instead of bespoke per-app scripts.

items.title: `b.cache` primitive
items.body: A framework-native cache primitive with pluggable backends, TTL, max-bytes ceiling, and observability signals â€” applications stop reaching for ad-hoc Map-based caches that grow unbounded.

releases.headline: Audit captures the 5 W's; success on by default when audit is wired

items.title: Audit envelopes capture the 5 W's
items.body: Every audit event now records who (actor), what (action), when (timestamp), where (request context / route), and why (outcome reason) â€” a uniform shape across every framework primitive that emits audit.

items.title: Success events on by default when audit is wired
items.body: Once an audit sink is registered, the framework emits success events by default â€” previously success was opt-in per primitive, leaving asymmetric coverage between failure and success.

items.title: API-key visibility holes closed
items.body: `b.apiKey` no longer leaks raw key material into log lines, error messages, or audit envelopes â€” the key is hashed once at issuance and only the prefix is surfaced to operator-readable output.

items.title: `auditSuccess` split into two distinct events
items.body: What was a single `auditSuccess` is now two events â€” a precursor emitted when the operation begins and a complete-event emitted on successful finish â€” so operators can distinguish started-but-not-yet-complete work from fully completed work in the audit stream.

items.title: `b.permissions` primitive
items.body: A first-class permissions primitive lets handlers declare required capabilities and the framework enforces them before the handler runs â€” replaces ad-hoc per-route role checks with a uniform model.

items.title: Observability signals backfilled across the framework
items.body: Primitives that did not yet emit metrics, traces, and audit events are brought up to the same observability baseline as the request lifecycle â€” queue, scheduler, storage, mail, websocket, and rate-limit all now surface uniform signals.

items.title: Versioned credential-hash envelope
items.body: Stored credential hashes now carry an explicit envelope version so the framework can roll its KDF parameters or hash algorithm forward without invalidating existing credentials â€” verifications transparently re-hash on next login under the new parameters.

items.title: `b.apiKey` primitive
items.body: API-key issuance, storage (hashed, never plaintext), rotation, and revocation are now a first-class primitive â€” applications stop rolling their own bearer-token table.

items.title: Repository logo assets
items.body: Logo artwork is committed under the repository for use by downstream documentation, package registries, and operator-facing surfaces.

items.title: `b.webhook` signing and verification
items.body: Outbound webhooks are signed with a framework-issued signature header and inbound webhook handlers verify the signature before invoking the callback â€” replay-protected, constant-time comparison, no per-app HMAC code.

items.title: `b.slug` primitive
items.body: URL-safe slug generation with Unicode-aware normalization, length caps, and reserved-word avoidance â€” applications no longer roll their own ad-hoc lowercase-and-dash function.

items.title: `b.retry` primitive
items.body: A composable retry primitive with exponential backoff, jitter, and a bounded attempt budget â€” callers wrap any async operation and get uniform retry semantics without rolling their own loop.

items.title: Shared test helpers consolidated under `test/helpers/`
items.body: Common mocks (`_mockReq`, `_mockRes`, `_bodyReq`, `_bodyRes`, `_streamingRes`, `setupTestDb`, `teardownTestDb`) are consolidated so test fixtures stop rolling their own copies and instead reuse one canonical implementation.

releases.headline: Input validation hardened at framework entry points

items.title: Config-time and entry-point input validation throws on bad input
items.body: Entry-point primitives such as `C.TIME.*`, `C.BYTES.*`, `protocolDispatcher.create`, and `defineClass`-built errors now throw `TypeError` on non-finite numbers, negative durations, or wrong-shape arguments so operator typos surface at boot instead of mid-request.

items.title: `defineClass.factory` replaces per-module `_err` wrappers
items.body: Per-module error wrappers (`_err`) are replaced with a single `defineClass.factory` builder so every framework error class is constructed uniformly with a stable `.name`, `.code`, and structured `.metadata` field.

items.title: `b.requestHelpers` primitive
items.body: Common request-shape readers (resolveRoute, clientIp, requestId, etc.) are consolidated into `b.requestHelpers` so middleware and handlers share one defensive implementation instead of rolling their own.

items.title: `metrics._normalizeLabelArg`
items.body: Metrics label arguments now flow through a single normalization helper so cardinality stays bounded and string/number/undefined inputs render to a consistent canonical shape.

items.title: `audit.safeEmit` consolidates per-module audit emitters
items.body: Per-module `_emit` wrappers across `lib/` are replaced with a single `audit.safeEmit` entry point â€” drop-silent on the hot path, uniform envelope shape, one place to extend audit-sink behavior.

items.title: `b.protocolDispatcher` primitive
items.body: A protocol-aware dispatcher routes incoming requests to the matching handler family (HTTP, WebSocket, queue worker) from a single entry point, replacing ad-hoc per-protocol wiring across earlier releases.

items.title: `b.observability.tap` primitive
items.body: A single tap point lets operators subscribe to every framework observability signal (metrics, traces, audit events) from one place instead of registering exporters against three different primitives.

releases.headline: `index.js` header lists the complete public surface

items.title: Public surface enumerated in `index.js` header
items.body: The top of `index.js` now lists every public namespace the framework ships so operators reading the entry-point file see the full surface at a glance instead of having to grep `lib/`.

releases.headline: Request middleware captures `statusCode` + promotes span to route template

items.title: Request middleware captures response `statusCode`
items.body: The request lifecycle middleware now records the final response status code on the audit/metric/trace surfaces so operators can correlate outcome with route + latency without instrumenting per-handler.

items.title: Tracing span named after route template
items.body: Spans are renamed from the concrete URL to the matched route template (using `req.routePattern` from the previous release) â€” cardinality stays bounded and traces group meaningfully.

items.title: `req.routePattern` populated by the router
items.body: The router now exposes the matched route template on `req.routePattern` so downstream middleware (metrics labels, tracing span names, audit events) can group requests by template instead of by concrete URL.

items.title: OAuth generic preset
items.body: `b.auth.oauth` gains a generic preset that covers any spec-compliant OAuth 2.0 / OIDC provider without a per-vendor adapter â€” operators supply the discovery URL and client credentials and the framework wires the rest.

items.title: Built-in metrics
items.body: `b.metrics` ships built-in counters/gauges/histograms for every framework primitive â€” request latency, queue depth, scheduler fire counts, audit-sink failures â€” surfaced without per-app instrumentation.

items.title: Framework tracing taps
items.body: `b.tracing` taps every framework primitive boundary so traces span the request lifecycle automatically; operators wire an exporter and get a full waterfall without per-handler code.

releases.headline: Storage POST-form presigning + logger deprecation warning

items.title: Storage POST-form policy presigning
items.body: `b.storage` gains presigned HTML POST-form policies â€” browsers can upload directly to the bucket via a standard `<form>` POST with the framework-issued policy document, complementing the existing presigned-URL flow.

items.title: Legacy logger surface
items.body: The standalone logger surface emits a deprecation warning pointing callers at `b.log`; removal is scheduled for a future minor release.

items.title: api-encrypt audit gaps closed
items.body: The `b.middleware.apiEncrypt` surface now emits audit events on every key-handling path â€” decrypt failures, mismatched envelope versions, and recipient-key mismatches all surface to the audit sink instead of being lost on the error path.

items.title: Cluster-shared nonce-store
items.body: Replay-protection nonces are now persisted in a cluster-shared store so a replayed request hitting a different worker is still rejected â€” single-worker memory was insufficient for multi-process deployments.

items.title: Audit handler isolation
items.body: Audit-sink handlers are isolated so a faulting handler can no longer take down the request that emitted the audit event â€” drop-silent on hot-path observability sinks.

items.title: `b.middleware.apiEncrypt` end-to-end PQC payload encryption
items.body: Request and response bodies can now be encrypted end-to-end with the framework's PQC envelope (ML-KEM-1024 + XChaCha20-Poly1305) so payloads stay confidential even past a terminating proxy â€” opt-in middleware that wires into the request lifecycle without per-route changes.

releases.headline: WebSocket channel/room hub with cluster pub/sub fan-out

items.title: WebSocket channel/room hub
items.body: `b.websocket` gains a channel/room hub so clients can subscribe to named topics; broadcasts to a topic fan out across the cluster via the existing `b.pubsub` backend instead of staying pinned to the worker that owns the socket.

items.title: Mail-bounce intake primitive
items.body: `b.mail.bounce` parses inbound DSN (Delivery Status Notification) messages and surfaces structured bounce events so callers can quarantine bad recipients without writing their own RFC 3464 parser.

releases.headline: Strip narrative comments from shipped source

items.title: Narrative comments stripped from shipped source
items.body: Source comments across `lib/` are rewritten to describe behavior instead of carrying internal-process narrative â€” operators reading the tarball see only operator-facing prose.

releases.headline: Logger folded into `b.log` + rate-limit cluster backend

items.title: Logger folded into `b.log`
items.body: The standalone logger surface is consolidated into `b.log` so the framework ships a single canonical logging primitive instead of two overlapping ones.

items.title: Cluster-shared rate-limit backend
items.body: `b.rateLimit` gains a cluster-shared backend so request counters survive across worker processes â€” a single client cannot evade limits by hashing onto a different worker.

releases.headline: Route raw time and byte literals through `C.TIME` / `C.BYTES` and type bare throws
releases.summary: Numeric literals representing durations and byte sizes are routed through the framework's `C.TIME.*` and `C.BYTES.*` constant tables so the operator-visible unit is named at the call site. Bare `throw new Error(...)` sites that crossed a public boundary are upgraded to typed framework error classes so callers can branch on `instanceof` rather than parsing strings.

items.title: Time and byte literals routed through `C.TIME` / `C.BYTES`
items.body: Hand-rolled `* 1000` / `* 1024` arithmetic across `lib/` is replaced with named constants from the central `C.TIME` and `C.BYTES` tables. Operators reading source see `C.TIME.MINUTE` instead of `60 * 1000`, and the codebase-patterns detector that refuses raw-literal multiplication keeps the discipline in place.

items.title: Bare `throw new Error(...)` upgraded to typed framework errors
items.body: Public-boundary throws that previously emitted plain `Error` objects now use the typed framework-error hierarchy (`TypeError` / `ValidationError` / `ConfigError` subclasses). Callers can branch on `instanceof` rather than matching message strings, and the typed errors carry a stable `name` for log-correlation.

items.title: Scheduler and presign gaps closed
items.body: Closes residual gaps in the scheduler exactly-once contract and the storage presigned-upload helper introduced in the previous release â€” tightens race-window handling and corrects edge cases in the signing surface.

items.title: Scheduler exactly-once across the cluster
items.body: `b.scheduler` now guarantees exactly-once execution globally rather than per-process, so a cron-style entry fires once across the entire cluster instead of once per worker.

items.title: Storage presigned upload URLs
items.body: `b.storage` exposes a presigning helper for direct browser-to-bucket uploads â€” the framework issues a short-lived signed URL the client uses without ever holding the master credential.

items.title: Queue lease-extension
items.body: Long-running job consumers can extend the visibility lease on an in-flight message instead of dropping it back into the queue when a single execution exceeds the original lease window.

items.title: Jobs dead-letter queue
items.body: `b.jobs` consumers that exhaust their retry budget now route the failing envelope into a dedicated dead-letter queue for operator inspection instead of silently dropping the work.
